Home › Find PD Courses › How K-12 Teachers Earn PD Credits, CEUs, and Salary Advancement with CE Credits Online How K-12 Teachers Earn PD Credits, CEUs, and Salary Advancement with CE Credits Online These interactive, self-paced courses are available anytime, anywhere – perfect for the busy teacher! With the skills learned, you'll be able to make an immediate impact in your classroom. Were you looking for? LAUSD Salary Points NYC DOE ASPDP Salary Differential Chicago Public Schools Lane Credit or browse all states Ready to browse courses? VIEW COURSES CE Credits Online offers easy and convenient online professional development courses for teachers. Our courses offer continuing education for teachers, teacher recertification, and salary advancement courses for teachers. Thousands of teachers have advanced their careers with CE Credits Online. See what they say → Jump to: How to get your credits | How to enroll | Common questions Quick Answer Complete any course and receive a Certificate of Completion Need an official transcript? Add university credits at purchase using the Type dropdown Most districts accept one or the other, check with your professional development department if unsure Self-paced and available 24/7 — complete on your schedule in 180 days How to Get Your PD Credits Once you successfully complete your course, we'll email you your Certificate of Completion. Most districts accept this as proof of professional development — it includes the course name and hours completed. University Credits If your district requires an official university transcript, you can add university credits when you purchase. Once you complete your course, we take care of the rest — we send all the required documentation directly to your chosen university, and they'll issue your official transcript and email you instructions for accessing it. Your transcript will include the course name, course number, number of credits, and your grade. Graduate-level credits are available through Adams State University, Augustana University, Valley City State University, and Cal Poly Humboldt. Visit our University Partners page to learn more. A note from us: Your success is important to us, and we want to make sure every course is the right fit for you. Credit acceptance varies by state and district, so if you have any doubt, check with your professional development department before purchasing — and feel free to contact us first. Refunds are not available if credits don't meet your requirements, and we'd rather help you choose right the first time. Not sure if your district will accept the credit? Most teachers download the course syllabus — you'll find the link right on the course page below the Add to Cart button — and share it with their professional development department. If you need anything else, we're happy to help. Just contact us. Professional Development Credits FAQs What credit types do CE Credits Online courses earn? Depending on your state and district, our courses can earn CEUs, Salary Points, In-Service Points, Lane Credits, Clock Hours, and Graduate-Level University Credits. Check the Credits Tab on any course page for details specific to your location. Do I need university credits or is a Certificate of Completion enough? Many teachers find the Certificate of Completion is all they need. If your district requires an official transcript, add university credits at purchase using the Type dropdown on the course page. Not sure? Check with your professional development department before purchasing. Can I add university credits after I purchase a course? University credits are easiest to add at purchase using the Type dropdown on the course page. If you completed a course and later find out you need university credits, contact us and we can help. Which universities are your partners? Graduate-level credits are available through Adams State University, Augustana University, Valley City State University, and Cal Poly Humboldt. Visit our University Partners page for more information. How long do I have to complete my course? You have 180 days (about 6 months) from the date of purchase to complete your course at your own pace. What if I'm not sure my district will accept the credits? Most teachers download the course syllabus — you'll find the link on the course page below the Add to Cart button — and share it with their professional development department. If you need anything else, contact us before you purchase and we're happy to help. Please note that refunds are not available if credits don't meet your requirements, and we'd rather help you choose right the first time. How to Enroll Go to our courses page and select the courses you wish to take On the course page, use the Type dropdown to select your credit option: Course Only — includes your Certificate of Completion University Credits — a second dropdown will appear to choose your university partner. Not all courses are available through all universities. Add the course to your cart and check out You will be asked to log in or create an account If you have a discount code, enter it at the end of checkout Upon purchase you will receive an email receipt and can log in to your course at eClassroom Sign in You have 180 days (about 6 months) from purchase to complete your course. VIEW COURSES Need help? Our support team can help you find the right courses for your district or state requirements. CONTACT SUPPORT Browse the courses for this program VIEW COURSES Still not sure which courses are right for you? Our support team can help you find courses that meet your district or state requirements. ← Browse all states CONTACT SUPPORT

children.headline: How K-12 Teachers Earn PD Credits, CEUs, and Salary Advancement with CE Credits Online
body: These interactive, self-paced courses are available anytime, anywhere – perfect for the busy teacher! With the skills learned, you'll be able to make an immediate impact in your classroom.

CE Credits Online offers easy and convenient online professional development courses for teachers. Our courses offer continuing education for teachers, teacher recertification, and salary advancement courses for teachers.
Thousands of teachers have advanced their careers with CE Credits Online. See what they say →

Once you successfully complete your course, we'll email you your Certificate of Completion. Most districts accept this as proof of professional development — it includes the course name and hours completed.

If your district requires an official university transcript, you can add university credits when you purchase. Once you complete your course, we take care of the rest — we send all the required documentation directly to your chosen university, and they'll issue your official transcript and email you instructions for accessing it. Your transcript will include the course name, course number, number of credits, and your grade.
Graduate-level credits are available through Adams State University, Augustana University, Valley City State University, and Cal Poly Humboldt. Visit our University Partners page to learn more.

A note from us: Your success is important to us, and we want to make sure every course is the right fit for you. Credit acceptance varies by state and district, so if you have any doubt, check with your professional development department before purchasing — and feel free to contact us first. Refunds are not available if credits don't meet your requirements, and we'd rather help you choose right the first time.

A note from us: Your success is important to us, and we want to make sure every course is the right fit for you. Credit acceptance varies by state and district, so if you have any doubt, check with your professional development department before purchasing — and feel free to contact us first. Refunds are not available if credits don't meet your requirements, and we'd rather help you choose right the first time.

Not sure if your district will accept the credit? Most teachers download the course syllabus — you'll find the link right on the course page below the Add to Cart button — and share it with their professional development department. If you need anything else, we're happy to help. Just contact us.

Depending on your state and district, our courses can earn CEUs, Salary Points, In-Service Points, Lane Credits, Clock Hours, and Graduate-Level University Credits. Check the Credits Tab on any course page for details specific to your location.

Many teachers find the Certificate of Completion is all they need. If your district requires an official transcript, add university credits at purchase using the Type dropdown on the course page. Not sure? Check with your professional development department before purchasing.
University credits are easiest to add at purchase using the Type dropdown on the course page. If you completed a course and later find out you need university credits, contact us and we can help.

Graduate-level credits are available through Adams State University, Augustana University, Valley City State University, and Cal Poly Humboldt. Visit our University Partners page for more information.
You have 180 days (about 6 months) from the date of purchase to complete your course at your own pace.

Most teachers download the course syllabus — you'll find the link on the course page below the Add to Cart button — and share it with their professional development department. If you need anything else, contact us before you purchase and we're happy to help. Please note that refunds are not available if credits don't meet your requirements, and we'd rather help you choose right the first time.
You have 180 days (about 6 months) from purchase to complete your course.
Our support team can help you find the right courses for your district or state requirements.

items: How K-12 Teachers Earn PD Credits, CEUs, and Salary Advancement with CE Credits Online
Complete any course and receive a Certificate of Completion
Need an official transcript? Add university credits at purchase using the Type dropdown
Most districts accept one or the other, check with your professional development department if unsure
Self-paced and available 24/7 — complete on your schedule in 180 days
Go to our courses page and select the courses you wish to take

On the course page, use the Type dropdown to select your credit option: Course Only — includes your Certificate of Completion University Credits — a second dropdown will appear to choose your university partner. Not all courses are available through all universities.
Add the course to your cart and check out
You will be asked to log in or create an account
If you have a discount code, enter it at the end of checkout
Upon purchase you will receive an email receipt and can log in to your course at eClassroom Sign in

landmarks.innerText: CE CREDITS ONLINE 23224 NE 156th Pl. Woodinville, WA 98077 Phone: 425.788.7275 support@cecreditsonline.org HAVE QUESTIONS? You can contact CE Credits Online by submitting a request from your Student Homepage under Customer Service which is in the upper right corner of your screen. You may also email us at support@cecreditsonline.org or call our customer service line at 425.788.7275 Ext. 104. Our office hours are Monday-Friday 8:00AM-5:00PM PST. We look forward to hearing from you!
